summary: Clean soundboard recording of a high‑energy New York show with standout Truckin', Mr. Charlie, Jack Straw and a soaring Jerry Garcia.
review: Fans rave about the vibrant performance – Jerry’s guitar tone on Mr. Charlie and Black Peter, Pigpen’s soulful moments in Mr. Charlie and Next Time You See Me, plus an electric Truckin' opener and a rare Run Rudolph Run. The Miller soundboard is described as clear and clean, though several listeners complain about unnecessary track splits that disrupt the flow.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give high marks (4‑5 stars) and praise both the musical set and the clean soundboard; only a few technical complaints about track splitting lower the score slightly.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear, crisp board with minimal noise; intro problems noted at the very start and many listeners object to the way Cryptical Envelopment/Drums/The Other One are split into separate tracks.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑octane opener (Truckin'), a mix of bluesy numbers and soulful Pigpen showcases, seamless segues into classic jams; occasional criticism of the Cryptical/Drums/The Other One break but overall energetic pacing.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s guitar tone praised on Mr. Charlie, Black Peter and other tunes; his solos described as soaring.
band_member_comments.Phil: One reviewer wanted Phil’s bass higher in the mix; his backing vocals noted on Jack Straw.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s vocal work highlighted on Jack Straw and Me And My Uncle.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboard playing called “nice” with a specific nod to Keith’s piano touch.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy’s drum solo seen as brief; several fans ask for more extended drum sections.
